section: 6.3.2 Which materials are hard? · Activity 6.5: Let us observe
audience: 11-12 year old students, Indian classroom context
learningObjective: Understand that hardness is a relative property. Sponge (soft), Rubber Eraser (medium), Iron Nail (hard). Students scratch each object virtually, record observations, and synthesise a ranking with a reason.

studentFriendlinessFeatures.language: Simple, warm, encouraging — Brilliant work, Whoa, Great job
studentFriendlinessFeatures.steps: Numbered step-by-step journey: Scratch then Record then Rank. Nothing unlocks until the prior step is done.
studentFriendlinessFeatures.feedback: Immediate animated feedback on every scratch. Fun facts appear after each scratch.
studentFriendlinessFeatures.cheer: CheerBanner appears after all scratched, after table filled, after submit.
studentFriendlinessFeatures.celebration: Confetti plus trophy plus result card with encouraging language on submit.
studentFriendlinessFeatures.textbookLink: Direct quote from NCERT page 107 shown at the bottom.

objects.name: Sponge
objects.scratchFeedback: Whoa! Your finger sinks right in! The sponge squishes super easily. It is very, very soft!
objects.funFact: Fun fact: Sponges have millions of tiny air pockets inside — that is why they squish so easily!

objects.name: Rubber Eraser
objects.scratchFeedback: It pushes back a little! The eraser bends and dents, but not as much as the sponge. It is soft-ish!
objects.funFact: Fun fact: Rubber comes from rubber trees! It stretches and bounces back — but iron cannot do that at all!

objects.name: Iron Nail
objects.scratchFeedback: It will not budge at all! The iron nail is rock-solid. You cannot dent it with your thumb. Very hard!
objects.funFact: Fun fact: Iron is used for bridges and tall buildings because it is so hard and strong!

activityFlow: Student reads the How It Works card — 3 steps explained simply
Student taps Tap to Scratch on each object card
2.3-second animation: emoji shakes, thumb wiggle icon, scratch particles burst out
Indent bar animates from 0 to calibrated depth (sponge=90%, eraser=45%, nail=5%)
Green feedback callout plus fun fact appear after each scratch
CheerBanner appears when all 3 are scratched
Table rows unlock — student picks Hard or Soft and types observation note
CheerBanner appears when table is complete
Synthesis section unlocks — student reorders ranking with up/down arrows
Submit: confetti, trophy, per-item correct/incorrect ticks, result card with learning summary

correctAnswers.keyInsight: Hardness is relative — rubber is harder than sponge but softer than iron (NCERT p.107)
